publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, research and lecture notes, catalogs, newspaper clippings, scripts, theatrical programs, photographs, audiotapes, manuscripts, and other papers.
did.abstract: Correspondence and other papers of Henry Salem Hubbell, a portrait artist and educator.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Henry Salem Hubbell, a portrait artist, was the head of the Carnegie Institute of Technology School of Painting and Decoration in Pittsburgh, 1918-1921.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Letters (1897-1907) from Hubbell, when an art student in Paris, Etaples, and Madrid, and from his wife, (Nellie) Rose Strong Hubbell, largely to his benefactor Mrs. Lydis Avery Coonley Ward; correspondence (1891-1944) between Hubbell and his family; research and lecture notes; correspondence, catalogs, and newspaper clippings concerning his paintings and exhibitions; scripts, programs, and notices of amateur plays in which he was either an actor or set designer; and photographs of himself and his paintings, studios, homes, and family. Also, tape recordings, notes, and correspondence of his son, Henry Willard Hubbell, concerning his father's life and work; typescripts and proofs of the journals, plays and short stories, articles, and verse written by Mrs. Hubbell; photocopies and typescripts of papers dealing with the history and genealogy of the Hubbell (Hubbels, Hubbel), Gleason, Rockwell, and related families (1800-1940); and proposals concerning the role of art education at the University of Miami.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Henry Salem Hubbell papers, #1955. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Hubbell, Nellie Rose Strong.
Painting.
Literature.
Education in art.
Art.
Amateur theater.
controlaccess.corpname: Carnegie Institute of Technology. School of Painting and Decoration

did.unittitle: #68: Secretaries of Interior Department - research material including list of the secretaries from 1849-1889 and vital statistics of each; pencil sketches of facial characteristics of several of the secretaries; correspondence
Copies of Account of Proceedings and Receipt and Release in the matter of the settlement of Trust Funds for Mr. and Mrs. Hubbell set up by W. Bingham 2nd, April 1949
Alice Gleason Bowman correspondence, newspaper clippings
Family correspondence - original and t.s. copies
Re: portrait of Mrs. J. G. Gehring
Newspaper clippings re: photos of paintings
Odds and Ends: real estate, will, miscellany
If I Could Fly by R. H. Hubbell - proof, reviews, clippings
Newsclippings - "Quacky Doodles" by R. S. Hubbell
"It Happened to Us" Memoirs of an Artist's Wife by R. S. Hubbell
Carnegie Tech School of the Arts - R. S. Hubbell
Transcripts of tapes B,C,D - recordings #2-8, p. 25-53
Transcripts of tapes B,C,D - recordings #2-8, p. 1-24
Photographs - Cornelia Gray Lunt, Hubbell Houses, and Cornell University
Artistic Anatomy of The Human Figure by Henry Warren
Photographs and negatives of: sketches of human anatomy at Carnegie Tech; paintings; Hubbell and Hubbell family; Strong family; friends; France and Spain; Chicago; also, scrapbook pages
Photographs of portraits and of paintings other than portraits
Printed material including art exhibit catalogs
Tape recordings made by Willard Hubbell of descriptive notes, personal comments, and reminiscences relating to the Hubbell papers
